description:
You are the on-call platform engineer responsible for keeping the in-house CI/CD
repository at

/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts

in a healthy, deploy-ready state.  
A new tagged build (v1.0.0) has just landed and you must perform a standard
“cut-over” procedure that consists of creating a brand-new release directory,
adding the release’s deployment script, rewiring a couple of symbolic links,
and recording everything in a cut-over log.

The following conventions are already in place inside
/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts:

1. releases/        – directory that contains one sub-directory per release
   (e.g. releases/v0.9, releases/v1.0.0, …).

2. current          – **symbolic link** that must *always* point to the directory
   of the release that production jobs should use.

3. shared/          – directory that stores resources common to every release
   (config files, secrets templates, etc.).  
   Every release must expose a symbolic link named config.yml that points back
   to shared/config.yml so that tasks that chdir into the release can still
   read the central configuration.

4. ci_cd_symlink_update.log – a cut-over log stored directly in /home/user that
   is used by downstream automation to verify that the symlink rotation
   completed successfully.

Your task
---------

Finish the cut-over for the new v1.0.0 build. Operate strictly inside the
/home/user directory tree; you do **not** have root access.

The required end-state is:

A. Directory structure & files
   A1. /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0  
       ├── deploy.sh          (executable shell script, see “File contents”.)  
       └── config.yml → ../../shared/config.yml
       (the arrow denotes a **symbolic link**, not a regular copy)

   A2. /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared  
       └── config.yml         (regular file, see “File contents”)

B. Symbolic links
   B1. /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/current  
       – must be a symbolic link that resolves to  
         /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0

   B2. /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0/config.yml  
       – must be a symbolic link (relative or absolute is fine) that resolves
         to /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml

C. File contents
   C1. /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0/deploy.sh  
       Exact text (including trailing newline):
       #!/usr/bin/env bash
       echo "Deploy v1.0.0"

   C2. /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml  
       Exact text (including trailing newline):
       app_env: production
       release: v1.0.0

D. Cut-over log
   D1. /home/user/ci_cd_symlink_update.log must be created (or appended) with
       **one** new line that documents this rotation.  
       The line must contain the four fields below separated by a single
       tab character (U+0009) and must end with a newline (U+000A):

       1) Static timestamp                     → 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z  
       2) Absolute path of the new release     → /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0  
       3) Absolute path stored in “current”    → /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0  
       4) Absolute path of shared/config.yml   → /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml

       The resulting single line must therefore be **exactly**

       2024-01-01T00:00:00Z	/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0	/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0	/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml

       (A literal tab character, not spaces, separates the fields.)

Important notes & constraints
-----------------------------
• The directory /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts already exists.  
  It currently contains:  
  – releases/v0.9/ (some legacy files)  
  – current → releases/v0.9 (symbolic link)

• No shared/ directory is present yet; create it as part of this task.

• The test harness will verify:
  – that the paths exist and are of the correct *type* (dir, regular file, or
    symbolic link);  
  – that every symbolic link resolves to the exact absolute target required;  
  – that the contents of deploy.sh and config.yml match exactly, byte-for-byte;  
  – that ci_cd_symlink_update.log contains the required single line at the end
    of the file.

• You are free to decide how you achieve the result, but do NOT hard-code any
  absolute path other than those specified above. Use robust, atomic steps so
  that “current” never points at a non-existent directory, even for a brief
  moment.

When everything is correct, simply exit the shell. The automated checker will
run and validate the artefacts described above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state *before* the agent starts
--------------------------------------------------
/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts
├── releases
│   └── v0.9
│       └── deploy.sh  (content irrelevant to test)
/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/current  →  releases/v0.9
(no shared/ directory yet)
/home/user/ci_cd_symlink_update.log does NOT exist yet.

Expected final state *after* successful completion
--------------------------------------------------
1. Directories
   /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0    (directory)
   /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared              (directory)

2. Regular files and their exact byte contents (with trailing newline):
   a) /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0/deploy.sh
      "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
      "echo \"Deploy v1.0.0\"\n"

   b) /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml
      "app_env: production\n"
      "release: v1.0.0\n"

3. Symbolic links and their targets:
   /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/current
        → /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0
   /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0/config.yml
        → /home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml

4. Log file
   /home/user/ci_cd_symlink_update.log (permissions 0644 or stricter)
   Exact single-line content (with trailing newline):
   "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\t/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0\t/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/releases/v1.0.0\t/home/user/repos/ci-cd-scripts/shared/config.yml\n"
